item.title: Yum! Brands sells struggling Pizza Hut in $2.7 billion deal
item.description: The sale will split ownership of the pizza chain between a U.S.-based private equity firm and a Chinese restaurant company.

item.title: Georgia and Alabama GOP runoffs and more primaries to watch today
item.description: Voters are casting their ballots in four states and the District of Columbia on Tuesday, including the runoff race in Georgia to take on Democratic Sen. Jon Ossoff.

item.title: Iran says Israel must pull troops out of Lebanon under deal with U.S.
item.description: Iran's foreign minister says Israeli troops can't remain in Lebanon under the pending deal with the U.S.

item.title: FBI disrupts alleged plot targeting White House UFC event, officials say
item.description: The FBI disrupted an alleged attempt to target the UFC America 250 event in Washington, D.C., FBI Director Kash Patel said Tuesday.

item.title: These U.S. cities have $1 million entry-level homes, a report finds. Is this the new norm?
item.description: A record 242 U.S. cities have entry-level homes priced at $1 million or more and just under half of the cities are in California, a new report from Zillow found. CBS News business analyst Jill Schlesinger breaks down housing market prices, mortgage rates and more.

item.title: Underdog Cabo Verde shocks Spain with 0-0 draw in historic World Cup debut
item.description: Another shocking upset stunned fans during the 2026 FIFA World Cup after Cabo Verde held their own against Spain, one of the world's most formidable teams. Stefano Fusaro, Jermaine Jones, and Michael Lahoud of CBS Sports Golazo Network react to the match and share what they're looking forward to.

item.title: What is the price of silver today?
item.description: Silver can play a critical role in a diversified portfolio. Here's how much it costs per ounce right now.

item.title: What is the price of gold today?
item.description: Understanding the pricing trends and dynamics of the gold market is crucial for making sound financial choices.

item.title: Trump "not happy" with Israel's handling of Hezbollah conflict in Lebanon, worried about Iran deal
item.description: Israel is under increased scrutiny after President Trump criticized Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's handling of the conflict against the Iran-backed Hezbollah militant group in Lebanon. CBS News' Imtiaz Tyab and Olivia Rinaldi report.

item.title: Uganda details safety measures as Ebola cases continue to spread in parts of central Africa
item.description: The total number of confirmed Ebola cases in central Africa has increased to more than 800, which is about 300 more than last week. Ramy Inocencio reports on the surge of cases and looks at how one country is trying to stop the spread.

item.title: SpaceX to buy AI coding assistant Cursor for $60 billion
item.description: The deal comes just days after SpaceX went public in the largest IPO in history, raising $75 billion to help fund its expansion.

item.title: The DOJ is investigating Gavin Newsom and his wife, he says. Here's what we know
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom says he's being investigated by the Justice Department, along with his wife. Chief Washington analyst Robert Costa breaks down what Newsom is saying and what's known about the probes.

item.title: Newsom says Justice Department is investigating him and his wife
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom, a Democrat, said the Justice Department is investigating he and his wife, Jennifer.

item.title: 8 killed in B-52 bomber crash at Air Force base in California
item.description: Eight people were killed when a B-52 bomber crashed shortly after takeoff Monday during a test flight at Edwards Air Force Base in California. The Air Force said the plane was carrying civilians and military personnel. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: Reader's guide for "Little Wonder" by Sophie Chen Keller
item.description: Here's a reader's guide for "Little Wonder" by Sophie Chen Keller

item.title: Oprah picks "Little Wonder" by Sophie Chen Keller for book club: Read an excerpt
item.description: Oprah Winfrey chose "Little Wonder" by Sophie Chen Keller as her latest book club pick. Read a free excerpt here.

item.title: Israel's recent attack in Lebanon was "too much," Trump says
item.description: President Trump, who is in France for the G7 summit, said he didn't like that Israel attacked Lebanon two hours before the U.S. signed an agreement with Iran.

item.title: Serena and Venus Williams to play doubles together again, at Wimbledon
item.description: Serena and Venus Williams are getting back together as a doubles team, at Wimbledon. The last time the sisters were a doubles duo was at the 2022 U.S. Open, where they lost their opening match​.

item.title: Mitch McConnell admitted to the hospital, spokesperson says
item.description: Sen. Mitch McConnell was admitted to the hospital Sunday morning, a spokesperson for the Republican confirmed to CBS News.

item.title: Iran World Cup team coach says it was booted from U.S. right after first match
item.description: Iran's World Cup team coach says it was ordered to leave the U.S. and return to its training base in Mexico only a few hours after opening its politically charged tournament with a draw.

item.title: 8 dead in B-52 bomber crash at Edwards Air Force Base in California, officials say
item.description: The aircraft was on a routine test mission at Edwards airfield, located in the western Mojave Desert, about 100 miles northeast of Los Angeles.

item.title: Iran, Israel voice caveats on deal ahead of expected signing ceremony
item.description: Senior U.S. officials say President Trump and Iran's top negotiator have already remotely signed a memorandum of understanding ahead of an expected signing ceremony.

item.title: BASE jumping accident kills 2 including well-known extreme athlete Andy Lewis
item.description: A BASE jumping accident in a Utah canyon killed two people including a daredevil athlete best known for performing onstage with Madonna at the 2012 Super Bowl, authorities said.

item.title: U.S. citizen arrested over alleged child sexual abuse in Colombia
item.description: A video of the alleged incident has circulated widely on social media, but Colombia's president later said the man "apparently did not" abuse any children.

item.title: Humpback whale caught in fishing rope freed by responders
item.description: A young humpback whale was freed from entanglement in fishing gear off the coast of Massachusetts on Sunday.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: American doctor who recovered from Ebola back in U.S., says he's "feeling well"
item.description: Dr. Peter Stafford, his wife, Rebekah Stafford, and their four children all arrived safely on Monday, according to Serge, a Pennsylvania-based Christian missions organization.

item.title: Cellphone fire on flight "scorched" cabin before landing in Las Vegas
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration said British Airways Flight 271 landed safely at Harry Reid International Airport in Las Vegas after the crew reported a cellphone fire on board.

item.title: California daycare remains open after children test positive for drugs, investigation finds
item.description: About half a dozen parents told CBS California Investigates they had not been informed of the daycare's probationary status, even though the state requires the facility to inform all current and prospective parents.

item.title: Referee denied entry into U.S. was talking to "very bad people," official says
item.description: Andrew Giuliani, the executive director of the White House's World Cup task force and the son of former NYC Mayor Rudy Giuliani, said he stood by the U.S.'s decision to reject Omar Artan.
